EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED TOUCHED AT FORT RUCKER. LARGE TREE LIMBS AND SMALL TREES WERE BROKEN OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH. A WOODEN DUGOUT STRUCTURE AT A BASEBALL FIELD WAS ALSO DAMAGED, ALONG WITH POWERLINES. MAXIMUM WINDS ARE ESTIMATED TO BE ABOUT 75MPH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR KINGSMAN STREET AND ANDREWS AVENUE AND MOVED NORTH- NORTHWEST, LIFTING NEAR BAKER STREET.

A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO WITH 105 MPH WINDS TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF HOBBY HILL ROAD AND MOVED NORTHEAST ACROSS MEDDERS ROAD BEFORE DISSIPATING AT HWY 33. MINOR DAMAGE WAS SUSTAINED TO A FEW MANUFACTURED HOMES AND MORE SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO AN OUTBUILDING.

BRIEF EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED AT 1:49 AM CST. MOST DAMAGE ALONG CHLOE CT WHERE A COUPLE OF HOMES SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE AND A CAR WAS FLIPPED. MAX WINDS ESTIMATED AT 85 MPH. PATH LENGTH 1.06 MILES. PATH WIDTH 75 YARDS. END POINT NEAR 31.2448,-85.4721.
HOUSTON EMA REPORTS A GARAGE WAS DESTROYED AND A VEHICLE FLIPPED OVER ON CHLOE CT. TIN ROOF WAS PEELED BACK ON A HOME. SEVERAL TREES AND POWER POLES SNAPPED.

AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN SOUTHEAST TIFT COUNTY NEAR TY TY SPARKS ROAD. THE TORNADO TRAVELED NORTHEAST ACROSS FLAT FORD ROAD AND KEITH FLETCHER ROAD AND LIFTED JUST AFTER CROSS TY TY OMEGA ROAD. A POWER SUBSTATION SUSTAINED DAMAGE. IN ADDITION, IRRIGATION SYSTEMS WERE OVERTURNED AND TREES WERE DOWNED.
